title: Drosanthe scabra
taxonomy: Drosanthe scabra is classified in the family Goodeniaceae. It belongs to the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Goodeniaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the Goodenia family. Drosanthe scabra is native to Western Australia. The species is native to the southwestern part of the continent.

Drosanthemum floribundum is a succulent plant species in the Aizoaceae family, commonly found in South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Drosanthemum floribundum include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including colds, fever, and digestive issues.
